items.summary: Emergency crews in Missouri rescued about 20 people on Friday after a building collapsed at a campground

items.title: Both engines flamed out before small jet crashed in June on highway, NTSB report says
items.summary: Both engines flamed out on a small business jet that crashed on a Texas highway in June, killing one person and injuring six, preventing the pilots from being able to reach the Laredo airport

items.title: Lo mejor del día: España y su victoria de último momento
items.summary: La ‘Roja’ sufrió ante Bélgica, en un partido que estaba empatado 1-1 y que se definió al 87’ tras un error del arquero, con un rebote que aprovechó Mikel Merino, mandando a guardar el balón y haciendo oficial unas semifinales soñadas: España vs Francia.

items.title: Telemundo Deportes celebra el legado de Tu Momento, Tu Jugada desde Rockefeller Center
items.summary: Desde el Fan Village de Telemundo, Luis Rosero destacó el impacto de Tu Momento, Tu Jugada, una iniciativa que recorrió más de 15 ciudades y llegó a más de 150,000 personas usando el poder del fútbol para unir e inspirar a la comunidad latina.

items.title: Conexión Mundial | Argentina busca otro paso hacia el sueño mundialista
items.summary: Los expertos repasaron la actualidad de Argentina antes del duelo frente a Suiza por los cuartos de final del Mundial 2026. Lionel Messi suma ocho goles en el torneo y la Albiceleste llega tras superar con carácter a Cabo Verde y Egipto.

items.title: Rudi Garcia tras eliminación de Bélgica: “Estoy orgulloso de mis jugadores”
items.summary: Rudi Garcia habló tras la eliminación de Bélgica y destacó el esfuerzo de su equipo pese a las dificultades. El entrenador aseguró que los Diablos Rojos compitieron de igual a igual ante España y agradeció el apoyo de todo el país.

items.title: Jugadores belgas vs. España: cámara exclusiva de todas sus acciones
items.summary: Revive el desempeño de Kevin De Bruyne, Jérémy Doku y Romelu Lukaku en la derrota de Bélgica ante España por los cuartos de final del Mundial 2026.

items.title: ¡Con la lengua fuera! La alocada celebración de Keyne que provocó la sonrisa de Lamine Yamal
items.summary: El hermano menor de Lamine Yamal volvió a robarse los reflectores, esta vez con una serie de gestos imposibles de pasar por alto que provocaron una divertida reacción del futbolista español.

items.title: Nico Williams está agradecido por su recuperación en un año complicado y sueña en grande con España
items.summary: El delantero del Athletic Club ha tenido que enfrentar problemas físicos esta temporada, pero ha logrado sobreponerse y se muestra agradecido y optimista por su participación con España en el Mundial; además, ve a Francia un rival complicado en semis.

items.title: Gomes: “Esto es un profesional: jugar los minutos que el entrenador le concede” | Todo el Mundial
items.summary: Aunque los cambios para Bélgica provocaron la pérdida de posesión del balón, en el caso español, Mikel Merino entró como un jugador que aprovechó las oportunidades y la sorpresa de la lesión de Courtois, afirman los expertos.

items.title: Conexión Mundial | Francia vs. España: ¿quién llega mejor?
items.summary: Los expertos debatieron quién llega mejor a la semifinal entre Francia y España. Mbappé suma ocho goles y Francia no muestra fisuras, pero la Roja mantiene su solidez y ya sabe lo que es vencer a los Bleus.

items.title: ¡España lo gana al final! Los Ángeles vibra con un cierre inolvidable
items.summary: Hinchas de España y Bélgica vivieron un partido inolvidable en Los Ángeles. La Roja consiguió el gol del triunfo en los minutos finales para avanzar a las semifinales de la Copa Mundial de la FIFA 2026, mientras la afición belga sufrio la derrota.

items.title: Graham Platner makes it official in Maine, submitting paperwork to leave Senate race
items.summary: In his withdrawal notice, Platner said "people are desperate for change" and that's why they made him the Democratic nominee. Now, Maine Democrats have to pick someone to replace him by July 27.

items.title: Gol de Mikel Merino y España vuelve a tomar la ventaja frente a Bélgica
items.summary: El mediocampista aprovecha un error del arquero Senne Lammens y de pierna izquierda marca el segundo de la “Roja” sobre los “Diablos Rojos”.

items.title: Graham Platner officially withdraws from the Maine Senate race
items.summary: Embattled candidate Graham Platner officially dropped out of Maine’s U.S.

items.title: Taylor Swift paid back NYC for wedding security, Mamdani says
items.summary: Singer Taylor Swift has paid back New York City for security and other costs tied to her lavish days-long Madison Square Garden wedding festivities the week of July 4, the city’s mayor said.

items.title: Patriotic art gets the spotlight as NEA funding shifts. Cue 'The Ronald Reagan Overture'
items.summary: Patriotic art and music is taking center stage this year under the Trump Administration, as funds shift away from DEI. For some orgs, like the Reagan Presidential Library, this is their wheelhouse.

items.title: Metallic space balls found on Australian beach
items.summary: Mysterious metal spheres washed ashore in Australia, and officials say they’re literal space balls — but with no known connection to Mel Brooks’ “Spaceballs.”

items.title: Houston neighbors started seeing more ICE agents around. Then came a fatal shooting.
items.summary: Lorenzo Salgado Araujo was a 52-year-old Mexican national who worked in construction for more than three decades. The father of three was shot by Immigration and Customs Enforcement agents after they attempted to pull him over.

items.title: County seeks help to care for 16 siblings from squalid home, prosecute their family
items.summary: The discovery of 16 siblings who authorities say were held at a home for years in squalid conditions is straining their rural Ohio county’s resources as it works to prosecute their parents and two grandparents and address the needs of so many children ...

items.title: Cyclosporiasis cases are climbing this summer — these products can help reduce your risk of infection
items.summary: Cyclosporiasis cases are rising across the US. Learn what causes this parasite, how to prevent it, and shop produce brushes and washes to keep it out of your house.

items.title: We want to hear about your experience with gambling addiction
items.summary: If you or a loved one has been affected by a gambling problem, NBC News wants to hear from you.

items.title: Family of Nolan Wells demand answers after he died during boating trip
items.summary: The family of Nolan Wells is asking for answers after the teenager was found dead during a boating trip in Mississippi. NBC News' Aaron Gilchrist reports on what the family had to say and the latest developments in the investigation.

items.title: ‘Real Housewives of Orange County’ star says son was involved in California brush fire
items.summary: “The Real Housewives of Orange County” star Jennifer Pedranti released a statement revealing her son was involved in the Ladera Ranch brush fire in California.

items.title: 86 people have been hospitalized with foodborne parasite spreading across the U.S., CDC says
items.summary: Nearly 3,000 people have been sickened with a gastrointestinal illness called cyclosporiasis, a foodborne infection marked by intense, watery diarrhea, state health officials report.

items.title: A major housing bill is set to become law at midnight — even though Trump says he won't sign
items.summary: President Trump says he is refusing to sign the bill without Congress first passing his sweeping voter ID bill.

items.title: U.S.-Iran fighting appears to pause. And, life inside Israel's military zones in Gaza
items.summary: Fighting between the U.S. and Iran appears to have paused after two days of clashing amid a shaky ceasefire. And, a look at what life is like inside Israel's expanding zone of control in Gaza.

items.title: Taliban declares war on smartphones
items.summary: A newly announced ban on smartphones for government workers, police and military personnel is spilling over into healthcare and educational facilities. Ordinary citizens worry they'll be next.

items.title: Wally Funk, aviation pioneer who was the oldest woman to travel into space, dies at 87
items.summary: Wally Funk, an aviation pioneer who was the oldest woman to launch into space, has died

items.title: Waymo called the cops on teen riders, raising privacy concerns
items.summary: Two 15-year-olds were allegedly drinking alcohol and shooting toy guns from a driverless taxi when the company disabled it and alerted police.

items.title: Count Binface: The intergalactic warrior who could upend Britain's strangest election
items.summary: Meet Count Binface: the challenger from another planet taking on Nigel Farage as questions over the Reform UK leader's finances overshadow his election comeback.

items.title: One U.S. visa program is growing rapidly. No one is happy with it
items.summary: Republicans on Capitol Hill are starting to talk about one facet of immigration reform: how to expand the popular H-2A visa program for farm laborers. They face obstacles.

items.title: 9 months into a ceasefire, Israel now controls nearly 70% of Gaza
items.summary: When the U.S. brokered a ceasefire last year, Israel controlled half of Gaza. Now Israeli forces have pushed deeper, and Palestinians are paying a deadly price.

items.title: Mexican man killed in Houston ICE shooting was not the target of operation, lawmaker says
items.summary: A Texas congresswoman says a Mexican man living in the U.S. who was fatally shot by an Immigration and Customs Enforcement agent was not the person federal authorities had been targeting in a Houston operation

items.title: Lawyers for man charged with killing Charlie Kirk question reliability of evidence
items.summary: Lawyers for the man accused of killing conservative activist Charlie Kirk plan to call a final witness on Friday as they try to raise doubts about the prosecution’s case

items.title: As the country turns 250, retired judges hit the road to defend judicial independence
items.summary: A group of retired federal and state judges has been barnstorming through Ohio and Pennsylvania on the nation's 250th anniversary to defend judicial independence and bolster the rule of law

items.title: Trump ousts remaining members of the Election Assistance Commission
items.summary: The dismissals hamstring a key bipartisan agency just months before the midterms.

items.title: Man charged with killing National Guard member hospitalized after refusing food
items.summary: Federal prosecutors say a man accused of shooting two National Guard troops near the White House, killing one of them, has been taken to a hospital for treatment of a potentially life-threatening medical condition after he refused to eat food in jail

items.title: Mbappé y Dembélé brillan con golazos en la victoria de Francia sobre Marruecos.
items.summary: Los cracks de Francia aparecieron en el momento clave. Kylian Mbappé y Ousmane Dembélé firmaron dos auténticos golazos para sellar el 2-0 sobre Marruecos en los Cuartos de Final.

items.title: Democrats in mad dash to replace Graham Platner before deadline
items.summary: Campaign volunteers have been taping over old Platner signs to quickly repurpose them for new candidates.

items.title: Judge dismisses lawsuit challenging Tuberville's eligibility to run for Alabama governor
items.summary: A judge has dismissed a lawsuit alleging U.S. Sen. Tommy Tuberville does not meet the residency requirement to run for governor of Alabama

items.summary: President Donald Trump's plan to build a triumphal arch that would alter the Washington, D.C., skyline is getting another review from the federal agency whose approval he needs to build it

items.title: Trump flies partway home from Turkey in an old Air Force One, not the new Qatari-gifted jet
items.summary: President Donald Trump has flown partway home from a NATO summit in Turkey on an old baby blue Air Force One plane instead of making the full trip aboard the new Qatari-gifted and retrofitted jet he arrived in

items.summary: The Maine Democratic Party has voted to hold a convention now that Graham Platner has announced he'll drop out of the U.S. Senate race

items.summary: The son of a Mexican national fatally shot by an Immigration and Customs Enforcement officer in Houston says he was a hardworking father who had been working toward a work permit after 35 years in the U.S. Lorenzo Salgado Araujo was driving to a constr...

items.title: Judge sets February trial for man charged with planting pipe bombs on the eve of the Capitol riot
items.summary: A federal trial is scheduled to start in February for a Virginia man charged with planting pipe bombs near the national headquarters of the Democratic and Republican parties in Washington on the eve of Capitol riot on Jan. 6, 2021

items.title: Mississippi residents in DeSoto County file lawsuit over majority-Black judicial subdistrict
items.summary: Several Mississippi residents in DeSoto County have filed a federal lawsuit against the creation of majority-Black subdistricts for the state judiciary

items.title: The Trump administration is ramping up pressure on states to change election practices
items.summary: The Trumps administration has been ramping up pressure on state election officials to make sure noncitizens aren't registered to vote

items.summary: A Federal Reserve rate-setting committee agreed to keep its key rate unchanged at its meeting last month, though most officials were split over whether inflation is likely to stay elevated or whether it will cool once the Iran war winds down, according...

items.title: Houston shooting marks at least the 8th fatality in US immigration sweeps
items.summary: A Houston man shot by immigration officers has become at least the eighth person to die during the Trump administration's immigration enforcement campaign

items.title: Some evacuation orders and street closures remain as work continues on a NYC high-rise that buckled
items.summary: Crews have worked overnight to shore up a massive, under-construction apartment building in Manhattan after columns buckled and floors sagged, triggering widespread evacuations and street closures over concerns about a collapse

items.title: Is an Iran deal ‘over’ and war back on? A timeline of the conflict and talks
items.summary: Global markets and oil prices have been shaken after the United States and Iran exchanged new strikes and President Donald Trump suggested that negotiations between the countries might be “over.”

items.title: Charges filed against Indian crime boss in assassination that strained Canada-India ties
items.summary: Authorities have charged the leader of an Indian criminal group in connection with the assassination of a prominent Sikh leader in Canada

items.title: US airlines’ monthly fuel spending topped $6B again in May, up 84% from year ago
items.summary: Government data released Tuesday shows that U.S. airlines spent $6.66 billion on jet fuel in May

items.title: Former NBA star Lamar Odom expected to enter plea deal in Las Vegas DUI case
items.summary: Former NBA star Lamar Odom is expected to enter a plea deal in a DUI case in Las Vegas

items.title: New Year's Eve ball drops in Times Square for America's 250th
items.summary: The New Year's Eve ball in Times Square has dropped for America's 250th anniversary, making this the first time the ball has dropped outside of a New Year's celebration.

items.title: The best shampoos for eliminating dandruff
items.summary: I spoke with dermatologists to round up the best dandruff shampoos including Head & Shoulders, Nizoral, Dove and more.

items.title: GOP prepared to block vote to replace Feinstein on Senate Judiciary
items.summary: Senate Majority Leader Chuck Schumer said on Tuesday that he hopes to replace Democratic Sen. Dianne Feinstein on the Senate Judiciary Committee with Sen. Ben Cardin of Maryland and aims to set up a floor vote on the issue this afternoon, which Republicans are expected to block.

items.title: Supreme Court seems sympathetic to postal worker who didn't work Sundays in dispute over religious accommodations
items.summary: The Supreme Court seemed to side with a former mail carrier, an evangelical Christian, who says the US Postal Service failed to accommodate his request to not work on Sundays.

items.title: Anti-abortion doctors urge Supreme Court to keep mifepristone restrictions in place
items.summary: A group of doctors opposed to abortion asked the Supreme Court Tuesday to restrict access to a key medication abortion drug while other legal challenges play out, as Wednesday night's deadline for the court to act approaches.

items.title: McCarthy makes plea for Republicans to back debt ceiling plan
items.summary: Speaker Kevin McCarthy made a plea to House Republicans during a closed-door meeting Tuesday morning to back his debt ceiling plan, telling them that although it doesn't have to include everything they want, it will help get him to the negotiating table with President Joe Biden.

items.title: Fulton County, Georgia, jail leadership resigns after inmate's death and accusations of unsanitary conditions
items.summary: Three officials at the Fulton County, Georgia, jail have stepped down amid an investigation into the death of an inmate whose family said was housed in a filthy, bug-infested cell that "was not fit for a diseased animal."

items.title: China's economy is off to a solid start, rising 4.5% in Q1 2023
items.summary: China's economy is off to a solid start in 2023 following its emergence from three years of strict pandemic restrictions.

items.title: He was free for 2 years. Now Crosley Green is back in prison for a crime he says he didn't commit
items.summary: A Florida man who served three decades behind bars for a murder he says he didn't commit returned to prison Monday after spending the past two years building a life outside prison walls.

items.title: Family of victim and survivors of Indianapolis FedEx mass shooting file lawsuit against gun magazine manufacturer and distributors
items.summary: The family of a victim and several survivors of a mass shooting at a FedEx facility in Indianapolis filed a lawsuit against companies involved in the manufacturing, marketing and sale of the high capacity magazine used by the gunman who killed 8 people and injured several others in 2021.

items.title: McDonald's is upgrading its burgers
items.summary: McDonald's, which has been focusing on upgrading its core items to boost sales, is rolling out a series of changes designed to improve its signature burgers.

items.title: Google-parent stock drops on fears it could lose search market share to AI-powered rivals
items.summary: Shares of Google-parent Alphabet fell more than 3% in early trading Monday after a report sparked concerns that its core search engine could lose market share to AI-powered rivals, including Microsoft's Bing.

items.title: Tennessee Air National Guardsman applied to be a hitman online, the FBI says. It was a spoof website and now he's facing charges
items.summary: A Tennessee Air National Guardsman is facing charges after applying to be a hitman on a spoof "rent-a-hitman" website, according to the Department of Justice.

items.title: Even when wives make as much as husbands, they still do more at home
items.summary: • Four out of the five US metro areas with the lowest unemployment are in Florida. Here's why • Opinion: The overlooked problem with raising the retirement age for Social Security
